description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical stone base and a wooden canopy supported by two vertical posts. The canopy features a pitched roof with red, tile-like shingles and wooden beams, providing shelter over the well opening. A hand crank mechanism is attached to one side, connected to a horizontal wooden rod, which would typically be used to lower and raise a bucket, though no bucket is visible. The stonework of the well is textured and slightly irregular, giving it a rustic appearance. Two flat stone slabs are placed on the ground nearby, possibly as stepping stones. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a red-tiled wooden canopy and a hand crank mechanism, featuring a cylindrical stone base and decorative wooden beams.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched red roof, wooden support beams, and the open stone well with two stone slabs on the ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hand crank mechanism and the horizontal rod above the well, with the stone base and canopy clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pitched roof and the stone well, with one stone slab visible on the ground.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the crank handle on the right, the wooden canopy, and the well's stone structure.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A hand crank and horizontal rod mechanism are present, made of wood and metal, but no bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A cylindrical stone wall surrounds the well opening, approximately waist-high, with a wooden canopy overhead supported by two posts.
